document.title: January 24, 2023 Meeting of the Antimicrobial Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document

key_concerns: Single phase 3 study with wide 20% noninferiority margin; did not meet 10% margin.
Neurotoxicity signal in nonhuman primates (tremors, Schwann cell hyperplasia, axonal degeneration).
Limited safety database of 151 patients, insufficient to detect rare adverse events.
Tremor imbalance in clinical data (2.6% vs 0% in comparator).
FDA disagrees with pooling phase 2 data to narrow the NI margin.
Unclear if rezafungin provides meaningful advantages beyond once-weekly dosing.
key_positives: Phase 3 study met primary endpoint of Day-30 ACM noninferiority with 20% margin.
Once-weekly dosing offers convenience over daily IV echinocandins.
Potential for lower DDI potential than azoles and possibly caspofungin.
In vitro activity against some echinocandin-resistant isolates (claimed by applicant).

key_concerns: Single phase 3 study with wide 20% noninferiority margin; did not meet 10% margin.
Neurotoxicity signal in nonhuman primates (tremors, Schwann cell hyperplasia, axonal degeneration).
Limited safety database of 151 patients, insufficient to detect rare adverse events.
Tremor imbalance in clinical data (2.6% vs 0% in comparator).
FDA disagrees with pooling phase 2 data to narrow the NI margin.
Unclear if rezafungin provides meaningful advantages beyond once-weekly dosing.
key_positives: Phase 3 study met primary endpoint of Day-30 ACM noninferiority with 20% margin.
Once-weekly dosing offers convenience over daily IV echinocandins.
Potential for lower DDI potential than azoles and possibly caspofungin.
In vitro activity against some echinocandin-resistant isolates (claimed by applicant).
evidence_notes: Phase 3 study: 'Day 30 ACM rate of 23.7% in rezafungin and 21.3% in caspofungin, difference 2.4% (95% CI -9.7%, 14.4%).'
FDA: 'does not agree with the Applicant’s proposal... to pool the efficacy data from phase 2 and phase 3 studies.'
Tremor imbalance: 'Tremor was observed in four (2.6%) subjects receiving... rezafungin... and in none... receiving the caspofungin comparator.'
Nonclinical: 'intention tremors... axonal degeneration... Schwann cell hyperplasia persisted through recovery.'
Review team: 'Applicant has not provided sufficient data to support... improved activity against Candida spp. with reduced susceptibility... or improved tissue penetration.'
raw.questions_framing: FDA asks if benefit-risk is favorable for patients with limited/no alternative options, seeking committee input on a potential limited use indication given the single trial and safety concerns.

signal.label: MIXED
rationale: Concerns: Single phase 3 study with wide 20% noninferiority margin; did not meet 10% margin.; Neurotoxicity signal in nonhuman primates (tremors, Schwann cell hyperplasia, axonal degeneration).; Limited safety database of 151 patients, insufficient to detect rare adverse events.; Tremor imbalance in clinical data (2.6% vs 0% in comparator).; FDA disagrees with pooling phase 2 data to narrow the NI margin.

Positives: Phase 3 study met primary endpoint of Day-30 ACM noninferiority with 20% margin.; Once-weekly dosing offers convenience over daily IV echinocandins.; Potential for lower DDI potential than azoles and possibly caspofungin.; In vitro activity against some echinocandin-resistant isolates (claimed by applicant).
